figures.caption: Figure 2: Representative dynamic-50% test rollouts on the CKD case study in which CMWM stays closer to the held-out future eGFR trajectory than GPT-5.5.

Table 1: KidneyOnline cohort characteristics, feature dimensions, and missingness for the CKD case study. Continuous variables are reported as mean (standard deviation, SD) or median [interquartile range, IQR]. MAP, mean arterial pressure.
Table 3: Training configuration of the best CMWM variant on the CKD case study (wide action encoder, communication embedding).

Figure 1: The ChronoMedicalWorld Model (CMWM) framework. State pathway: a periodic clinical state xt together with the static patient embedding bi is mapped by the state encoder Es into the latent state zt. Action pathway: structured intervention indicators am t and a semantic embedding ac t of within-period patient–health-coach communication are mapped jointly by the wide action encoder Ea into the action embedding ut. The recurrent transition module G (implemented as a gated recurrent unit, GRU, in our case-study instantiation) propagates the fused stream [zt; ut] into a hidden state ht; the head H consumes [ht; bi; τ̃t+1] and produces both the next-period target observable ŷt+1 (e.g., eGFR, HbA1c, ejection fraction) and the next predicted latent ẑt+1. The static context bi is shown twice for layout clarity (left of Es and below H); it denotes the same input fed to both modules. The perimeter dashed loop indicates the autoregressive rollout used at evaluation, where ŷt+1 replaces the corresponding slot of the next-period clinical state.
